translations.descricao: Asthma is a chronic inflammatory disease of the airways characterized by recurrent episodes of wheezing, dyspnea, chest tightness, and cough, with variability over time and spontaneous or treatment-related reversibility. Early management is essential for symptom control and prevention of exacerbations. In the Brazilian context, the SUS Clinical Protocol emphasizes access to medications and patient education.
translations.sus.indicacao: Diagnosis and management of patients with suspected or confirmed asthma, including control assessment and crisis prevention. Recommended for all patients with persistent or recurrent respiratory symptoms.
translations.sus.populacaoAlvo: Children, adolescents, and adults with symptoms such as nocturnal cough, wheezing, and dyspnea; prioritizing risk groups such as allergic individuals and smokers.
translations.sociedadesMedicas.indicacao: The Global Initiative for Asthma (GINA 2024) recommends diagnosis based on clinical history and pulmonary function tests, with stepped management for control. The Brazilian Society of Pulmonology and Tisiology (SBPT 2021) emphasizes a personalized approach and the use of ICS from the beginning.
translations.epidemiologia.prevalencia: Global prevalence varies from 1% to 18% in different populations. In Brazil, it is estimated at about 10% in urban adults.
translations.epidemiologia.incidencia: The global annual incidence is 5-10 cases per 1,000 people. In Brazil, approximately 3-5% per year in children.
translations.epidemiologia.mortalidade: Global mortality is low, about 0.1-0.2 per 100,000 inhabitants, but underreported in developing countries. In Brazil, it contributes to 2-3% of deaths from respiratory diseases.
